handoffs.changes:
- Added tools/hygiene_check.py: read-only stdlib CLI with stale workflows, stale capsules, phantom invariants, incomplete handoffs, and dangling approval_requests checks; detailed markdown and summary-only outputs; exit 0/1/2 semantics.
- Updated .cursor/hooks/orchestrator_guard.py: added fail-open daily hygiene reminder using tools/hygiene_check.py --summary-only and .cursor/hooks/.hygiene_last_check.txt state.
- Updated .cursor/rules/90_multiagent_workflow.mdc: documented Daily hygiene check trigger, manual run, human-in-the-loop policy, and CI-friendly exit behavior.
handoffs.assumptions:
- Hook summary parser treats script exit code 1 as valid when stdout contains a summary, because findings intentionally return 1; Risks if false: reminder would be suppressed whenever hygiene finds rot.
- Existing modified config/capsules_manifest.json content is user/current-branch state; Risks if false: synthetic restore evidence could be confused with pre-existing manifest diff.
- No full simulation, ClickHouse, or BI validation was needed because this is process/tooling hygiene infrastructure; Risks if false: unrelated runtime behavior remains untested.

handoffs.changes:
P0.2 Agent KG split: code/utils/agent_kg.py now saves atomically with os.replace and read-state falls back to config/agent_kg_archive; tools/kg_migrate_archive.py created and executed; old closed workflows archived to JSONL.
P0.3 audit hygiene: .cursor/hooks/audit_code_edit.py and .cursor/hooks/user_comm_audit.py write hash-chained JSONL entries; tools/audit_verify.py and tools/audit_summarize.py created; docs/audit_summaries/ created with README and generated 2026-05-15 summary.
handoffs.facts:
KG migration dry-run: 2,044,976 -> 218,145 bytes projected. Execute: 2,044,976 -> 218,145 bytes actual; retained 20 closed + 1 active/nonclosed; archived 186 workflows, 670 handoffs, 211 contexts into 186 JSONL files across 4 date dirs.
Agent KG smoke: init_workflow PASS, write_handoff PASS, read_state PASS, close_workflow PASS on temp KG; archived read_state fallback PASS.
Audit smoke: py_compile PASS for changed Python files; code_edit audit verify OK: 2 entries verified (148 legacy lines skipped); user_comm audit verify OK: 1 entry verified (397 legacy lines skipped); audit_summarize generated docs/audit_summaries/2026-05-15.md with OK hash status.
ReadLints: no linter errors for edited Python/hook files.
handoffs.assumptions:
Existing pre-hash audit log lines are legacy local data; verifier skips legacy non-JSON lines and verifies the new hash-chain entries. Risks if false: a strict all-lines-JSON interpretation would require one-time legacy log conversion, not requested in P0.3.
No POSIX flock was added; atomic os.replace prevents torn writes, but concurrent writers can still have last-writer-wins behavior. Risks if false: high-concurrency Agent KG writes may still need a follow-up lock layer.

handoffs.risks: R1 (low): if Algorithm A1 wrong, hidden out-of-scope modification to 90_multiagent_workflow.mdc could be masked as workflow-internal drift. Mitigation вАФ orchestrator inspects git diff before close. R2 (low): manifest.yaml classification (L1 vs L3) may need refinement after framework reuse in another project; not blocking initial release. R3 (low): version_check.py is informational (no hard-block); drift detection requires manual orchestrator action вАФ acceptable per design (orchestrator/human decision per the rule bullet). R4 (very low): caps still under 25% вАФ no risk of cap exhaustion in current workflow.
handoffs.open_questions: N1: orchestrator should decide before pre_close whether to (a) run 'python3 tools/version_check.py --update' to refresh baseline for the rule he just edited, or (b) explicitly document workflow-internal drift in close handoff. Either is acceptable; not blocking. N2: approval phrase entry at T10:44:40Z has workflow_id_source=none (init was POST-approval, not PRE per Tier-1 F2 expectation). Soft WARNING from orchestrator_guard expected; downstream prompts inherit inferred_approval_context from registered KG context. Not blocking medium-risk dispatch per .cursor/rules/90_multiagent_workflow.mdc C2 (approval_request context exists in KG). N3: pre_close discipline вАФ orchestrator must include DriftCheck and GraphUpdate=no fields in final close handoff (no Domain Graph sync involved in Tier-3 scope). N4: docs-curator handoff was Handoff-lite low-risk (changelog update only); acceptable per .cursor/rules/91_handoff_template.mdc; no escalation needed.

- tools/kg_to_otel.py: Agent KG + optional archive to OTel JSON resourceSpans, workflow root spans, handoff child spans, context events, workflow filter, pretty output, 10MB chunking.
- tools/security_smoke.py: lite runner for regex/static checks and hook subprocess simulations only.
- tools/pii_scan.py: regex PII scanner for KG workflows/handoffs/contexts, optional logs, handoffs-only and exit-on-finding modes.
- tests/security/01..08_*.json: 8 prompt injection / plan deviation / tool misuse / caps / secrets / SSoT smoke cases.

handoffs.changes: Review-only. No file writes. Examined git diff of code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py (+63/-0 lines) and surrounding context L1-L40 (top-level imports) and L1750-L1850 (main() before/after insertion point).
handoffs.evidence:
Facts: Dispatch SuccessCriteria checked: 'KG-guard insertion does not touch RTC/CUDA/simulation lifecycle/CLI'. Evidence supporting ACCEPT: (i) git diff confirms additive-only edit at single insertion point L1763-L1825; (ii) source read of L1-L40 confirms no top-level import changes; (iii) source read of L1750-L1850 confirms call site precedes all CUDA-related calls in main(); (iv) nested function uses stdlib-only local imports; (v) sys.exit(2) on failure path executes before any pyflamegpu/CUDA initialization. ACCEPT (pass). Verdict: pass.
Evidence: 1) Diff scope: addition of nested function _kg_guard() at L1764-L1823 + single call site _kg_guard() at L1825; no deletions; +63 lines, 1 file (within minimal-diff cap <=3 files, <=150 lines). 2) Top-level imports unchanged (L26-L29: import os, sys, time, argparse) вАФ no new GPU/CUDA modules introduced. 3) Local imports inside _kg_guard scoped to nested function: 'import json' (L1765), 'from datetime import datetime, timezone' (L1766), 'from pathlib import Path' (L1767) вАФ stdlib only, zero overlap with pyflamegpu/cuda/cupy/torch. 4) Call site _kg_guard() at L1825 is placed AFTER args = parser.parse_args() (L1762) and BEFORE 'print(LIMITER V8 вАФ –Р—А—Е–Є—В–µ–Ї—В—Г—А–∞ —Б RepairLine)' (L1827-L1829), get_client() (L1832-L1833), DROP/CREATE TABLE (L1835-L1842) and ALL downstream simulation construction вАФ build_model(), fg.CUDASimulation(self.model), populate_agents(), simulate() are reached only after _kg_guard() returns success. 5) On failure path (file missing / JSON invalid / no active workflow) the guard calls sys.exit(2) BEFORE any FLAME GPU host call вЖТ no CUDA context created вЖТ no RTC kernel cache touched вЖТ exit is clean. 6) Function reads only: env vars (KG_GUARD_BYPASS, AGENT_KG_WORKFLOW_ID via os.environ.get) and file config/agent_kg.json (read-only via Path.read_text). No writes to filesystem, no mutation of global state, no new module-level identifiers. 7) CLI argparse contract preserved: parser is built at L1756-L1760 with original flags --version-date, --end-day, --max-steps, --drop-table; _kg_guard does NOT touch parser or args object. 8) RTC kernel functions (rtc_state_transitions_v7/v8, rtc_quota_v7, rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7) are defined elsewhere in the file (not in the diff range) and remain byte-identical. 9) Capsule/invariants: no INV-* / TEMP-* / GPU-* invariants touched (guard runs on host before GPU init; cannot violate GPU-1..GPU-6 race/memory constraints). 10) Surgical/Simplicity review: every added line traces to UserGoal (KG-guard enforcement); no orphan cleanup; no drive-by refactors; no speculative flexibility (single BYPASS env, single env override, single file lookup вАФ all explicitly requested by SuccessCriteria); fail-fast via sys.exit(2) with explicit diagnostic, no silent fallback. Verdict: pass. Notes (non-blocking): (a) datetime.fromisoformat('Z' -> '+00:00') replacement is compatible with Python 3.10+; remains correct on 3.11+. (b) If timestamp field is malformed, ValueError will propagate as uncaught exception вАФ acceptable fail-fast per project rule against silent try/except. (c) active_window_sec=3600 hardcoded; matches docstring '60 min' вАФ could be promoted to a module constant later if reused, but not required by current SuccessCriteria. Verdict: pass.

handoffs.risks:
- One-CAS/no-retry behavior can leave a lower-priority candidate waiting if its deterministic source1/bank position is invalidated by cross-phase P2/P3 interaction; regression must check ops deficits and claim counts.
- Candidate position is per commit phase, not shared across P2 and P3; P3 after P2 must be reviewed for source1 collision behavior.
- Bank fallback is deterministic for one head window per line per step, but full correctness still depends on regression over claim_source=2 rows and TEMP-5.

(2) git diff --stat –њ–Њ config/transitions/ + invariants.json –њ—Г—Б—В вАФ SSoT –љ–µ–њ—А–Є–Ї–Њ—Б–љ–Њ–≤–µ–љ–µ–љ. (3) Diff —Б–Њ–і–µ—А–ґ–∞—В–µ–ї—М–љ–Њ: —Г–±—А–∞–љ guard idx==0 –Є for i<RTC_MAX_FRAMES, –і–Њ–±–∞–≤–ї–µ–љ—Л 24 [idx].exchange(0u) + inline –Ш–Э–Т–Р–†–Ш–Р–Э–Ґ-–Ї–Њ–Љ–Љ–µ–љ—В–∞—А–Є–є (self-clear —В—А–µ–±—Г–µ—В –Њ—В—Б—Г—В—Б—В–≤–Є—П flamegpu::DEAD/setAllowAgentDeath(True) –Є append-only spawn; –Є–љ–∞—З–µ rollback –Ї clear-all/orphan cleanup) вАФ surgical, scope-matched. (4) KG read-state: 2 handoff –≤ workflow (coder-flame I1 + reviewer-flame R1 APPROVE_WITH_NOTES); approval_gate context recorded approved source=current_chat_user_prompt; phase_start –Њ—В governance –Ј–∞–њ–Є—Б–∞–љ —Н—В–Є–Љ –≤—Л–Ј–Њ–≤–Њ–Љ. (5) Research-graph-analyst handoff –≤ KG –і–ї—П —Н—В–Њ–≥–Њ workflow –Ю–Ґ–°–£–Ґ–°–Ґ–Т–£–Х–Ґ (grep handoff_W_sim_reset_parallel_selfclear вЖТ 2 —Б–Њ–≤–њ–∞–і–µ–љ–Є—П); validator-judge handoff –Ґ–Р–Ъ–Ц–Х –Ю–Ґ–°–£–Ґ–°–Ґ–Т–£–Х–Ґ; docs-curator handoff –Ю–Ґ–°–£–Ґ–°–Ґ–Т–£–Х–Ґ. (6) Regression evidence (version_id=8008 vs –і–µ—В–µ—А–Љ–Є–љ–Є—А–Њ–≤–∞–љ–љ—Л–є —Н—В–∞–ї–Њ–љ 8005, 4 –і–∞—В–∞—Б–µ—В–∞ √Ч 3650 –і–љ–µ–є, EXCEPT-both-ways=0 –њ–Њ sim_masterv2_v9 346979 —Б—В—А–Њ–Ї –Є sim_repairline_v9 262800, ops=target PASS –љ–∞ –≤—Б–µ—Е 4) –Є nsys-–њ–µ—А—Д (rtc_reset_quota_v8 105.6msвЖТ3.74ms ~28√Ч) —Ж–Є—В–Є—А—Г—О—В—Б—П –≤ changelog –Є user-prompt, –љ–Њ –Э–Х –Ј–∞—Д–Є–Ї—Б–Є—А–Њ–≤–∞–љ—Л –Ї–∞–Ї validator-judge handoff –≤ KG. (7) RiskTier=high –Є HumanGateRequired=yes —Б–Њ–≥–ї–∞—Б–Њ–≤–∞–љ—Л —Б ComplianceChecklist –Њ–±–Њ–Є—Е subagent handoffs; approval_ref –њ—А–Є—Б—Г—В—Б—В–≤—Г–µ—В –≤ –Њ–±–Њ–Є—Е. (8) reviewer-flame compliance_checklist: decision=allow_with_notes, required_gates=pass, exceptions=full_runtime_regression_pending_orchestrator вАФ exception —З–∞—Б—В–Є—З–љ–Њ —Б–љ—П—В bit-identical —А–µ–≥—А–µ—Б—Б–Є–µ–є (evidence –љ–µ –≤ KG, –љ–Њ —Б–Њ–і–µ—А–ґ–∞—В–µ–ї—М–љ–Њ strong). (9) Bit-identical —А–µ–≥—А–µ—Б—Б–Є—П –љ–∞ –і–µ—В–µ—А–Љ–Є–љ–Є—А–Њ–≤–∞–љ–љ–Њ–Љ —Н—В–∞–ї–Њ–љ–µ 8005 –љ–∞ 4 –і–∞—В–∞—Б–µ—В–∞—Е √Ч 3650 –і–љ—П—Е substantively –і–Њ–Ї–∞–Ј—Л–≤–∞–µ—В —Д—Г–љ–Ї—Ж–Є–Њ–љ–∞–ї—М–љ—Г—О —Н–Ї–≤–Є–≤–∞–ї–µ–љ—В–љ–Њ—Б—В—М parallel self-clear vs serial clear-all вАФ —Н—В–Њ —Б–Є–ї—М–љ–µ–є—И–Є–є –≤–Њ–Ј–Љ–Њ–ґ–љ—Л–є validator-evidence; —Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞–ї—М–љ—Л–є gap вАФ —В–Њ–ї—М–Ї–Њ –Ј–∞–њ–Є—Б—М –≤ KG. (10) Pre-existing dirty tree —Д–∞–є–ї—Л (.cursor/agents/**, hooks, rules) –і–Њ–ї–ґ–љ—Л –Ї–Њ–Љ–Љ–Є—В–Є—В—М—Б—П –Њ—В–і–µ–ї—М–љ—Л–Љ workflow вАФ –љ–µ —Б–Љ–µ—И–Є–≤–∞—В—М —Б —Н—В–Є–Љ –Ї–Њ–Љ–Љ–Є—В–Њ–Љ (anti-mixed-workflow rule).

code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:
- Removed old lines 765-768: HF_SpawnDiag wiring (`self._hf_spawn_diag`, `layer_spawn_diag`, `addHostFunction`).
- Removed old lines 863-866: `layer_sync_day_v5` registration and V8-side HF_SyncDayV5 registration print.
- Removed old lines 1052-1063: `self.hf_sync_v5.get_step_log()` consumer and reason-count print block.
- Removed old lines 1636-1662: `HF_SpawnDiag` class definition.
- Kept `rtc_limiter_v5.register_v5(...)` call at current lines 840-850, with comment clarified: `HF_InitV5` remains needed via `model.addInitFunction(hf_init)`.
handoffs.assumptions:
- Assumption: `HF_InitV5` side effects are still required for V5 compatibility init even though SyncDay layer is removed. Risks if false: register_v5 could be further simplified later, but removing it now might break initialization.
- Assumption: Removing final SyncDay env-sync is behaviorally inert because HF_StepController already updates env at step start and no later layer reads env after the removed final layer. Risks if false: regression run would catch stale end-of-step env visibility.
handoffs.evidence:
Facts: - `rtc_limiter_v5.register_v5` inspected: lines 516-518 create `HF_InitV5(program_changes, end_day)` and call `model.addInitFunction(hf_init)`; lines 521-538 only create/return `HF_SyncDayV5` when `sync_as_layer=True`, without registering it as step function. Therefore V8 keeps `register_v5` to preserve init behavior and leaves `self.hf_sync_v5` as an unused returned attribute.
- `register_v5_final_layers` remains intentionally not called in V8, preserving the existing guard against V5 compute_global_min overwriting V8 adaptive result.
- Full simulation was not run per user constraint; regression is owned by orchestrator.

- `/home/albud/miniconda3/envs/cuda13/bin/python3 -m py_compile code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py` -> PASS (exit code 0, no output).
- ReadLints on `code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py` -> No linter errors found.
- `git diff -- code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py` reviewed: one code file changed; no StepController/count/QM/transitions/MP2/RL drain/deterministic spawn edits.

handoffs.assumptions:
- Assumption: HF_InitV5 side effects are still required for V5 compatibility init even though SyncDay layer is removed. Risks if false: register_v5 could be further simplified later, but removing it now might break initialization.

handoffs.evidence:
Facts: Selective stash preserved config/agent_kg.json (verified: git status kept it M throughout). Schema toggled correctly: pre-extract md_components had ONLY partno_comp, post-restore has ONLY partseqno_i. Env conda cuda13 + load_env.sh + CUBE_CONFIG_PATH. version_date in sim tables UInt32 YYYYMMDD.
Evidence: COUNTS sim_masterv2_v9 version_id=1 version_date=20250704: baseline(other epoch)=85798; POST-rename acceptance(multi-dataset extract)=87226 x2 (run1, prior step B); POST-rename isolated re-extract(new code)=87275 x5 (trials 3-7 consecutive identical); PRE-rename isolated re-extract(old code)=87591 x1. md_components fingerprint PRE(col partno_comp) vs POST(col partseqno_i): rows77 nn76 uniq76 s_psn3585640 s_oh8 7627320 s_oh17 9727320 s_ll8 35754720 s_ll17 31807320 => IDENTICAL 8/8 (only column NAME differs). ETL row-level determinism: 2 consecutive fresh new-code extracts gave IDENTICAL fine content hash=6456267096678994565 (s_status=39084 s_rd=5884 s_addr=57189315) => ETL deterministic at row content level. SIM determinism on fixed heli_pandas: trials 3-7 = 87275 five times => sim deterministic on fixed input. PRE-rename run_all 20250704: TOTAL=15 FAIL=2 (INV-2 FAIL + INV-12 FAIL same acn=22490); POST-rename earlier was FAIL=1 (only INV-12). RESTORE: git stash pop OK (22 code files M + config/agent_kg.json present); grep shows partseqno_i back in md_components_enricher.py; DB md_components only partseqno_i, rows=77 s_psn=3585640.
handoffs.risk_reasons: A/B test toggles code via selective git stash + re-extract + re-sim on live ClickHouse; conclusion drives acceptance verdict of high-risk rename.

handoffs.risks: VERDICT rename equivalence = INCONCLUSIVE by single-count: count_A(pre)=87591, post=87275(isolated)/87226(acceptance), baseline=85798 => 3+ distinct values. BUT md_components data content pre==post byte-identical => rename is data-content-neutral at dictionary level. Count is extract-context-sensitive (87226 acceptance multi-ds vs 87275 isolated single-ds) so it is NOT a clean equivalence oracle; pre/post count delta is confounded. Prior-turn claim 'sim count is stable epoch artifact' is refined: within a fixed extract sim+ETL are deterministic, but counts shift across extract contexts.
handoffs.open_questions: To settle rename equivalence cleanly: capture heli_pandas fine content-hash for pre-rename extract vs post (if identical AND sim deterministic, any count diff = genuine sim rename effect; if differ, rename changed ETL output). Why does acceptance multi-dataset extract yield 87226 while isolated single-dataset re-extract yields 87275 (md_components dictionary build order / shared-table context)? Should a seeded/deterministic sim mode be used for bit-identical acceptance instead of raw counts?

(2) –Т–Њ–Ј–Љ–Њ–ґ–љ–∞ –≤–Є–Ј—Г–∞–ї—М–љ–∞—П –њ—Г—В–∞–љ–Є—Ж–∞: stacked bar —Б 6 —Б—В–∞—В—Г—Б–Њ–≤ √Ч 2 —В–Є–њ–∞ = 12 —Б–µ—А–Є–є, –Љ–Њ–ґ–µ—В –њ–µ—А–µ–≥—А—Г–ґ–∞—В—М –ї–µ–≥–µ–љ–і—Г –Є –Њ—Б–Є. –†–µ—И–µ–љ–Є–µ –њ–Њ semantic refinement вАФ –Ј–∞ –Р–ї–µ–Ї—Б–µ–µ–Љ (R3 –µ—Б–ї–Є –±—Г–і–µ—В –њ—А–Є–љ—П—В–Њ).

Filters repointed: 7 вАФ '–Т–µ—А—Б–Є—П –і–∞—В–∞—Б–µ—В–∞ 1' (col=version_date_ddmmyyyy), '–У—А–∞–љ—Г–ї—П—А–љ–Њ—Б—В—М 1' (no col, timegrain), '–Ґ–Є–њ –Т–° 1' (col=group_by_label), '–Т–µ—А—Б–Є—П –і–∞—В–∞—Б–µ—В–∞ 2' (col=version_date_ddmmyyyy), '–У—А–∞–љ—Г–ї—П—А–љ–Њ—Б—В—М 2' (no col), '–Ґ–Є–њ –Т–° 2' (col=group_by_label), '–У—А–∞–љ—Г–ї—П—А–љ–Њ—Б—В—М –Я–Њ—Б—В–∞–≤–Ї–Є' (no col). Filters intact (alive targets): '–Я–Њ—Б—В–∞–≤–Ї–∞: –≤–µ—А—Б–Є—П –і–∞—В–∞—Б–µ—В–∞' (datasetId=1 col=version_date_ddmmyyyy), '–У—А–∞—Д–Є–Ї —А–µ–Љ–Њ–љ—В–∞' (datasetId=3 col=version_date_ddmmyyyy). POST-PUT GET verify: 0 targets —Г–Ї–∞–Ј—Л–≤–∞—О—В –љ–∞ id=2 (dead), 7 –љ–∞ id=9 (must=7), 1 –љ–∞ id=1, 1 –љ–∞ id=3 вАФ –≤—Б–µ –ґ–Є–≤—Л–µ. handoffs.user_goal: Workflow W_dwh_analytics_load, branch feature/dwh-bb8, PlanStep P10: replay applicable enrichment cascade for heli_pandas 2026-06-12 v1 without re-running dual_loader/Excel loaders, rerun heli_pandas_ops_other_groups, target OPS planers > 0 and qualitatively close to 2026-04-08 baseline.
handoffs.changes:
1) Replayed existing planner processors in version scope only: status_overhaul -> program_ac -> inactive_planery, applying 286 planner-row updates (statuses plus overhaul dates for active repairs).
2) Ran scoped post steps as-is: program_ac_precheck_runner (0 changes), heli_pandas_component_status, heli_pandas_serviceable_status, heli_pandas_storage_status, heli_pandas_terminal_br_gate.
3) Intentionally skipped enrich_heli_pandas.py and heli_pandas_group_by_enricher.py because they mutate all versions; skipped heli_pandas_repair_status.py because its planner branch would overwrite inactive planers with status_id=2 for past target_date on this dataset.
handoffs.assumptions: Skipping global ac_type_mask/group_by enrichment is acceptable for P10 because target metric is planner OPS recovery, not full cross-version normalization. Risks if false: downstream BI/reporting that depends on ac_type_mask typing may still show mixed/unknown buckets until a separately approved scoped/global enrichment pass is run.
handoffs.evidence:
Facts: Before: planers status distribution was [(1,0,163),(2,0,123)], OPS planers=0, total status_id=0 rows=11540. After replay: planner distribution became [(1,1,95),(1,2,68),(2,1,15),(2,2,101),(2,4,7)], OPS planers=169, planner status_id=0=0, total status_id=0 rows=0. Final completeness rerun succeeded: code/heli_pandas_ops_other_groups.py reported 169 planers in status_id=2 and 5785 aggregates on OPS planers. Qualitative comparison to 2026-04-08 baseline (170 OPS) is near-equal by OPS count.
Evidence: Commands: in-memory replay simulation via existing processor functions; scoped execution of code/extract/program_ac_precheck_runner.py, heli_pandas_component_status.py, heli_pandas_serviceable_status.py, heli_pandas_storage_status.py, heli_pandas_terminal_br_gate.py; final rerun of code/heli_pandas_ops_other_groups.py. SQL facts: before OPS=0 / planner_zero=286; after OPS=169 / planner_zero=0 / total_zero=0.
handoffs.risk_reasons: Version-scoped ClickHouse mutations on production-like ETL tables; required selective replay because some existing extract steps are unsafe or unscoped for this dataset.
handoffs.plan_card: Replay safe planner-status cascade for 2026-06-12 v1 using existing processor logic without dual_loader rerun, then run scoped post-enrichment scripts that do not overwrite recovered planner statuses.
handoffs.success_criteria: numeric: OPS planers 0 -> 169 (>0) and manual-check: python3 code/heli_pandas_ops_other_groups.py --version-date 2026-06-12 --version-id 1 --skip-md --skip-pdf reports 169 status_id=2 planers; planner status_id=0 count becomes 0.
handoffs.risks: 1) ac_type_mask coverage remains incomplete, so completeness report buckets OPS planers as mixed/unknown. 2) heli_pandas_repair_status.py remains unsafe to replay blindly on this version because of planner-wide past-target override. 3) Planner replay used existing processor functions via one-off scoped script rather than a dedicated maintained runner.

handoffs.assumptions:
No hidden semantic fallback was added; old processor try/except behavior outside the new apply_* functions remains where it already existed. Risks if false: legacy swallowed exceptions in the first 3 planner processors may still mask upstream data issues, but this task did not alter those processors.
Standalone program_ac_precheck_runner has no historical --dry-run option, so smoke used compile plus full cascade execution rather than standalone no-op run. Risks if false: direct standalone invocation still mutates via one replace-slice, but the same path was exercised through checkpoint A.

handoffs.changes: No code logic changed. Runtime/env actions only: verified human-approved version_id-aware fix in code/sim_env_setup.py (preload_mp4_by_day/preload_mp5_maps + inline avg fl query at line 688 now filter by version_id; fetch_versions(2026-06-22)->vid=2). Reset .rtc_cache (backed up, then restored to original 546 files) as a runtime retry. Did NOT edit RTC/GPU code (coder-flame zone). Captured verbose JIT log to output/p8_sim/sim_verbose.log.
handoffs.assumptions: Root cause is a GPU JIT toolchain regression (most likely the minor NVIDIA driver update 580.142->580.159.03) breaking PTX->SASS link of a specific complex function condition for sm_120, while trivial kernels still link. Risks if false: issue may instead be a FLAMEGPU/nvjitlink interaction with --generate-line-info on this kernel; needs coder-flame RTC investigation.
handoffs.evidence:
fetch_versions(2026-06-22)=(2026-06-22, vid=2) confirmed. No stale vid=2 sim slice before run; after failed runs still none: sim_masterv2_v9=[(1,76559)], sim_repairline_v9=[(1,65700)], sim_masterv2_v9_daily=[(1,43800)], sim_deficit_v9_daily=[] (no vid=2 rows; crash occurred before any write -> no duplicates).
SIM run command: /home/albud/miniconda3/envs/cuda13/bin/python3 code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py --version-date 2026-06-22 --version-id 2 --end-day 3650 --max-steps 10000 (AGENT_KG_WORKFLOW_ID=W_spawn_limit_deficit_20260623, CUDA_PATH/LD_LIBRARY_PATH per rule). Timing: ~7-9s each (fails fast at model load), 2 attempts (live cache + fresh cache) identical failure.
FAILURE (exact): pyflamegpu FLAMEGPURuntimeException (InvalidAgentFunc) JitifyCache.cu(479): Error loading agent function (or function condition) 'rtc_count_inactive_v8': function had link errors: Linking failed: NVJITLINK_ERROR_PTX_COMPILE; Linker options: "-arch=sm_120 --generate-line-info". Crash site: orchestrator_limiter_v8.run -> _populate_agents -> agent_population.populate_agents -> setPopulationData (model load, before sim step; data-independent).
Environment diagnosis: pyflamegpu=2.0.0rc4+cuda130; _pyflamegpu.so links libnvrtc.so.13 from conda; system cuda-12.6 only provides libnvJitLink.so.12 (soname .13 required -> conda nvjitlink 13.0.88 used, NOT system). CUDA_PATH=conda targets; LD_LIBRARY_PATH conda-first. RTC smoketest (code/utils/rtc_smoketest.py) PASS = OK (trivial RTC function links sm_120 fine). .rtc_cache homogeneous prefix 13000_120_1 (546 files, no toolkit mixing). Driver now 580.159.03 / CUDA 13.0 (rule 15 documented 580.142) -> minor driver update since last successful sim (vid=1 cache dated Jun 17). rtc_count_inactive_v8 defined in code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_quota_v8_base.py + rtc_quota_v8.py, which are NOT in the modified-files set (feature/spawn_limit changes are in orchestrator_limiter_v8.py, rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7.py, sim_daily_materializer.py).

handoffs.assumptions:
No official runtime flag exists to suppress line-info in this pyflamegpu wheel; risk if false: undocumented env may exist upstream but is absent from local Python API, package strings, and docs checked.
Minimal repro passing means full-model generated RTC header/context is involved; risk if false: cache key/source mismatch could mask exact failing variant, but cache-off full run still fails.
handoffs.evidence:
Diagnosis: rtc_count_inactive_v8 source is simple: no recursion, no switch, no loops, no special intrinsics; it reads idx/group_by/status_change_day and writes two UInt32 MacroProperty slots plus one total counter. Full sim still fails before first simulation step at CUDASimulation.setPopulationData with NVJITLINK_ERROR_PTX_COMPILE and Linker options: "-arch=sm_120 --generate-line-info".
Source of --generate-line-info: no project-level setting found except FLAMEGPU_RTC_EXPORT_CACHE_PATH cache path. pyflamegpu public SimulationConfig exposes only log/steps/verbosity/timing/telemetry; CUDASimulation_Config exposes device_id/inLayerConcurrency only. Binary string inspection of _pyflamegpu.so shows Jitify/JitifyCache contains "-Werror --compile-only --generate-line-info" and linker option "--generate-line-info"; no runtime env switch found for line-info. FLAMEGPU docs/search show runtime env vars for FLAMEGPU_INC_DIR and FLAMEGPU_RTC_INCLUDE_DIRS, and build/CMake options for FLAMEGPU_RTC_EXPORT_SOURCES, FLAMEGPU_RTC_DISK_CACHE, FLAMEGPU_VERBOSE_PTXAS; no documented runtime flag to disable line-info.
Mitigation attempts: output/p8_sim/p9_env_lineinfo_test.log: ran full sim with FLAMEGPU_RTC_DISK_CACHE=OFF, FLAMEGPU_RTC_EXPORT_SOURCES=ON, FLAMEGPU_VERBOSE_PTXAS=ON, FLAMEGPU_RTC_GENERATE_LINE_INFO=OFF, FLAMEGPU_RTC_LINE_INFO=OFF, JITIFY_GENERATE_LINE_INFO=0; rc=1, same Linker options with --generate-line-info. output/p8_sim/p9_jitify_cache_off.log: programmatically set fg.JitifyCache.getInstance().useMemoryCache(False), useDiskCache(False), clearMemoryCache(); rc=1, same failure. Therefore cache corruption/stale disk cache is excluded.
Isolation: code/utils/rtc_smoketest.py PASS. output/p8_sim/p9_minimal_count_inactive_repro.py PASS with same RTC_COUNT_INACTIVE source, same conda CUDA13 env, minimal model with required variables/MacroProperties. Therefore rtc_count_inactive_v8 source alone and line-info alone are not sufficient to reproduce; failure depends on full FLAMEGPU generated dynamic header/model context under current driver/toolchain.
Environment facts retained from task and checks: pyflamegpu=2.0.0rc4+cuda130 from conda; CUDA_PATH=/home/albud/miniconda3/targets/x86_64-linux; LD_LIBRARY_PATH includes /home/albud/miniconda3/lib; conda libnvrtc/nvjitlink are used; trivial RTC and minimal count_inactive repro pass.
Result: safe code/env mitigation not found. GPU sim did NOT start successfully.
handoffs.risk_reasons: code/sim_v2 GPU runtime blocker; full V8 model cannot JIT-link RTC under current driver/toolchain; mitigation may require driver/toolkit/FLAMEGPU package change
handoffs.plan_card: Diagnose rtc_count_inactive_v8 JIT link failure; try safe env/cache mitigations; isolate with minimal PyFLAMEGPU repro; do not change quota semantics.

Read-only ClickHouse check (24.10.1.2812, version_date UInt32 filter 20260622, version_id=2) found target_count=0 in sim_masterv2_v9, sim_repairline_v9, and sim_deficit_v9_daily; sim_deficit_v9_daily is not filled for target scope. No invariant verdict was made.

handoffs.evidence:
Facts: Verified dispatch SuccessCriteria focus points 1-7. (1) Month reset persisted: write-back of spawn_this_month_mi17/spawn_month_mi17 is OUTSIDE if(need_17>0) block, gated only on spawn_limit_active==1 (rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7.py:324-327); counter cannot stick when need_17==0. No early-return between spawn_limit block and write-back. (2) Indexing: target_day==write_day==current_day (clamped); cap/active/index read at target_day, ticket reads need_mp[safe_day]=current_day - consistent with mp4 target and old code. (3) Type contract OK: host newMacroPropertyUInt for all three (orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:569-571), device getMacroProperty<unsigned int,MAX_DAYS> (rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7.py:292-294), HF getMacroPropertyUInt - NO UInt32 mismatch. (4) Sentinel spawn_month_mi17=0xFFFFFFFF in register (:636) and init (:725); month_index 0-based ordinal so sentinel never collides -> reset on first active day. (5) Full removal confirmed via grep: cumulative_dynamic_spawn_mi17/spawn_limit_cumulative/HF_InitSpawnLimitCumulative/spawn_limit_running_total absent from runtime code (only validation inv13 references cumulative, out of scope). (6) RMW safe: SpawnDynamicMgr single agent (init pushes 1); per-agent vars inherently non-racing. (7) Semantics match spec: month_active==0 skips clamp (unlimited); month_active==1 & cap==0 -> allowed=0 hard zero; deterministic spawn untouched; spawn_limit_active==0 fully disables clamp. No Float64, fail-fast bounds checks in HF init, no hidden try/except.
Evidence: Static review + grep. rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7.py:288-327 (mgr_v8 monthly clamp+reset), :636/:725 (sentinel), :623 (cumulative MP removed). orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:557-600 (env MacroProperty UInt + HF wiring), :486-497 (month-boundary deterministic_dates), :1621-1652 (HF_InitSpawnLimitMonthly bounds-checked). sim_env_setup.py:644-661 (month_cap/active/index build), :259-281 (preload month_active). grep: no dangling cumulative refs in runtime code. ReadLints: no errors. py_compile PASS (coder-flame). Full sim PASS: 263 steps, 82695 master rows, no NVJITLINK/DeviceError; SQL Jul=0<=2 Aug=0<=1 Sep=0<=0.

- Initial workbook had 120 monthly columns (`2025-01`..`2034-12`). Loader creates 4000 days for `version_date=2026-06-22` (`2026-06-22`..`2037-06-03`), so I added 30 monthly columns (`2035-01`..`2037-06`) to make v2 `spawn_limit_month_active=1` true for all 4000 days using xlsx data only. Total monthly columns after preparation: 150.
- Step 1/v2: set numeric `0` in row `spawn_limit` for all 150 monthly columns; left `new_counter_mi17` unchanged; ran direct AC loader for `version_id=2`.
- Step 2/v3: cleared row `spawn_limit` and row `new_counter_mi17` across all 150 monthly columns; ran direct AC loader for `version_id=3`.

handoffs.evidence:
Environment/context: `AGENT_KG_WORKFLOW_ID=W_spawn_limit_3tests_20260625`; conda `cuda13`; `set +u; source config/load_env.sh; set -u`; `CUBE_CONFIG_PATH=$PWD/config`; KG phase_start context `ctx_W_spawn_limit_3tests_20260625_phase_start_06273f97`.

ClickHouse: native `clickhouse_driver`, server `SELECT version()` = `24.10.1.2812`; `flight_program_ac` schema checked: `dates Date`, `new_counter_mi17 UInt8`, `spawn_limit UInt16`, `spawn_limit_month_active UInt8`, `spawn_limit_active UInt8`, `version_date Date`, `version_id UInt8`. `flight_program_ac` has no `group_by` column, so group_by filter is not applicable for this table.

SNAPSHOT before edits, `Program_heli.xlsx` sheet `2025`, initial monthly columns 120, rows: `–У–Њ–і` row 2, `new_counter_mi17` row 5, `spawn_limit` row 6. `new_counter_mi17` non-empty cells: `L / 2025-10 = 6`; `AA / 2027-01 = 6`; `AM / 2028-01 = 7`; `AY / 2029-01 = 8`; `BK / 2030-01 = 7`. `spawn_limit` non-empty cells: `CB / 2031-06 = 2`; `CR / 2032-10 = 3`; `DO / 2034-09 = 0`.

Extract commands: `python3 code/extract/program_ac_direct_loader.py --dataset-path data_input/source_data/v_2026-04-08 --version-date 2026-06-22 --version-id 2`; `python3 code/extract/program_ac_direct_loader.py --dataset-path data_input/source_data/v_2026-04-08 --version-date 2026-06-22 --version-id 3`. Loader generated/inserted 4000 rows for both v2 and v3. v2 log: `new_counter: 1 records / 5 columns`, `spawn_limit: 1 record / 150 limited months`. v3 log: `new_counter: 0 records`, `spawn_limit: 1 record / 0 limited months`. Trigger correction warning occurred for v2/v3 because `heli_pandas` is empty for these version_ids; main AC tensor load and loader validation succeeded.

SQL summary used: `SELECT version_id, count() AS rows, min(dates), max(dates), countIf(spawn_limit=0), countIf(spawn_limit!=0), sum(spawn_limit_month_active), min(spawn_limit_month_active), max(spawn_limit_month_active), min(spawn_limit_active), max(spawn_limit_active), sum(new_counter_mi17), countIf(new_counter_mi17!=0) FROM flight_program_ac WHERE version_date=toDate('2026-06-22') AND version_id IN (1,2,3) GROUP BY version_id ORDER BY version_id;`
Result: v1 `(rows=4000, dates=2026-06-22..2037-06-03, spawn_zero=4000, spawn_nonzero=0, month_active_days=0, min/max_month_active=0/0, min/max_active=0/0, new_sum=28, new_nonzero_days=4)`; v2 `(rows=4000, dates=2026-06-22..2037-06-03, spawn_zero=4000, spawn_nonzero=0, month_active_days=4000, min/max_month_active=1/1, min/max_active=1/1, new_sum=28, new_nonzero_days=4)`; v3 `(rows=4000, dates=2026-06-22..2037-06-03, spawn_zero=4000, spawn_nonzero=0, month_active_days=0, min/max_month_active=0/0, min/max_active=0/0, new_sum=0, new_nonzero_days=0)`.

Duplicates SQL: `SELECT version_id, count() AS duplicate_dates FROM (SELECT version_id, dates FROM flight_program_ac WHERE version_date=toDate('2026-06-22') AND version_id IN (1,2,3) GROUP BY version_id, dates HAVING count()>1) GROUP BY version_id ORDER BY version_id;` Result: empty set => duplicate_dates=0 for v1/v2/v3.

New-counter SQL: `SELECT version_id, dates, new_counter_mi17 FROM flight_program_ac WHERE version_date=toDate('2026-06-22') AND version_id IN (1,2,3) AND new_counter_mi17 != 0 ORDER BY version_id, dates;` Result: v1 and v2 both have `(2027-01-15,6)`, `(2028-01-15,7)`, `(2029-01-15,8)`, `(2030-01-15,7)`; v3 has no rows. Snapshot cell `2025-10=6` is outside the loader calendar starting at `2026-06-22`, so it correctly does not appear in DB.

Strict checks: v2 bad rows SQL `SELECT count() FROM flight_program_ac WHERE version_date=toDate('2026-06-22') AND version_id=2 AND NOT (spawn_limit=0 AND spawn_limit_month_active=1 AND spawn_limit_active=1);` => `0`. v3 bad rows SQL `SELECT count() FROM flight_program_ac WHERE version_date=toDate('2026-06-22') AND version_id=3 AND NOT (spawn_limit_active=0 AND spawn_limit_month_active=0 AND new_counter_mi17=0);` => `0`.

v1 untouched: pre-load baseline v1 was `rows=4000`, `new_sum=28`, `new_nonzero_days=4`, `spawn_limit_active=0`, duplicates=0, same four nonzero new-counter dates; post-load summary for v1 matches exactly. No v1 loader command was run.

Final xlsx check after Step 2: total monthly columns 150 (`2025-01`..`2037-06`); `final_new_counter_nonempty=[]`; `final_spawn_limit_nonempty=[]`.

handoffs.assumptions: The exported sim_* tables are accepted as the evidence source even though final flight_program_ac was overwritten by the last direct-loader run. Risks if false: rerunning sim for v1/v2 without reloading their fixture state would not reproduce the recorded v1/v2 outputs. The observed spawn_limit_active behavior is a current-pipeline regression rather than an intentional semantic change. Risks if false: expected values supplied by the user are no longer valid for commit c1b90656.

handoffs.evidence: Code evidence: AgentPopulationBuilder maps initial status_id=4 directly to FLAME state repair and sets pre_status_id=status_id, repair_days from MP3, exit_date=max(0, repair_time-repair_days) (agent_population.py lines 147-168, 226-243, 323-338). _init_repair_lines_at_build creates repair_quota RepairLine agents and assigns day0 repair population to first lines, free_days=0 in AgentVector plus day0_map (orchestrator_limiter_v8.py lines 1108-1143). HF_InitRepairLines then writes MacroProperty mp_days initial_free_days=rt_eff-remaining_days, mp_acn=acn, mp_rt=rt_eff for day0 lines (lines 1664-1747), so day0 repair IS registered, but not with free_days=0 for the whole occupied interval: for 22517 initial free_days=32. rtc_repair_line_increment increments free_days by adaptive_days even while acn!=0 and releases only by setting aircraft_number=0 when free_days>=rt; it does not reset free_days on release (rtc_repair_lines_v8.py lines 45-85). QuotaManager/source1/P3 eligibility reads repair_line_acn/free_days, counts today_committable when line_acn==0 && free_days>=line_rt, and P3 chooses best source1 line where line_acn_ro==0 and line_mp_ro>=repair_time; claim_start=current_day-repair_time (rtc_quota_v8.py lines 83-108, 365-445, 820-971). SQL evidence: CH 24.10.1.2812; sim_masterv2_v9 rows=73056; sim_repairline_v9 v3 rows=0; sim_deficit_v9_daily v3 rows=0. min_day=1. day0 status4 claimless counts group_by=1:0, group_by=2:3, claimed_status4=0; claim rows at min_day=0. day0 claimless aircraft: 22517 repair_days=32 repair_time=224 remaining=192 source=0 line=65535; 22419 repair_days=117 repair_time=122 remaining=5; 22485 repair_days=26 repair_time=218 remaining=192. Target: 22517 status4 through day188, exits at day192 pre=4 status=3; 25485 source1 claim at day344 line0 [164,344), commit_p3=1. Therefore line0 backdated claim starts 28 days before 22517 master repair episode closes ([164,192)).
handoffs.plan_card: Read-only continuation diagnostic: verify whether day-0 repair lines are registered and where RepairLine availability diverges from expected domain model.

handoffs.evidence:
Facts: VERDICT=ACCEPT (approve). Dispatch SuccessCriteria evidence reviewed for correctness (numeric PASS to be confirmed by validator-judge).
(1) CAP-INJECTION CORRECT+ENSEMBLE-SAFE:
 (a) spawn_cap_n read per-run: _make_runs sets run.setPropertyUInt('spawn_cap_n', cap) + setPropertyUInt8('spawn_limit_active',...) per RunPlan; HF reads env.getPropertyUInt('spawn_cap_n'); CUDAEnsemble gives each run isolated env copy -> per-run read correct.
 (b) on-device cumulative == synth_cumulative(threshold,days,cap_n): HF detects threshold = first-nonzero index of shared build array (synth_cumulative(threshold,days,1)), then mp[i]=0 if i<threshold else cap_n. Identical step shape to single-run/Layer-1 (run_one_cap builds env spawn_limit_cumulative=synth_cumulative(threshold,days,cap)). Build array uses cap=1 (not 0) so threshold detection still works for cap0 runs -> correct design.
 (c) no cross-run leakage: spawnlim_inited reset to 0 per RunPlan; HF early-returns if set; HF holds no mutable self state (reads immutable list + per-run env/macroprop) -> stateless. Determinism vid1160==vid1060 EXCEPT=0 (master+repairline) empirically confirms no leakage between concurrent runs.
 (d) threshold constant: derived once via get_reference_shape(ref_vid=9) at build, baked into shared array; only cap_n varies per run.
 Cross-check 1060==vid9 bit-identical (master+repairline EXCEPT=0) proves ensemble synthesis reproduces canonical real single-run reference (vid9: planned_spawn=0, spawn_limit_active=1, sum=60) byte-for-byte -> threshold+cap+full pipeline correct for cap60. Intermediate caps verified via births=cap + monotonicity (INV-13 consistent).
(2) SINGLE-RUN NON-REGRESSION: else-branch preserves original loop verbatim; ensemble branch gated by env ensemble_mode!=0 which defaults 0 (newPropertyUInt ensemble_mode 0) and set 1 only via RunPlan. Single-run (CUDASimulation) never sets it -> original path untouched. Confirmed by diff.
(3) PLANNED-SPAWN-DISABLE CORRECT: harness sets mp4_new_counter_mi17_seed=[0]*days AND _deterministic_spawn_count=0 before build_model, then re-calls _collect_deterministic_dates() so spawn days excluded from adaptive dates. build_model spawn_schedule empty -> HF_DeterministicSpawn NOT registered (no det agents, no off-by-one). All births dynamic (0->2), counted by INV-13; det spawn (0->3) excluded per INV-13 notes. Index layout reproduces vid9 exactly (bit-identical) -> no idx corruption in practice.
(4) UNCAPPED (vid1061) SEMANTICS CORRECT: rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7 gates cap by if spawn_limit_active==1u; with =0 cap block skipped, spawn bounded only by dynamic_reserve_mi17 -> 61 births. Matches INV-13 spawn_limit_active==0 PASS by definition.
(5) ENSEMBLE-SAFE/FAIL-FAST: HF stateless; fail-fast RuntimeError when no positive reference threshold or values exceed MacroProperty; harness fail-fast on days_total mismatch, duplicate version_id, subprocess check=True. No silent fallback/hardcode.
(6) B3b READINESS: no code blockers. Peak VRAM governed by concurrent_runs (=2 streamed batches), not total run count -> scaling subset->62 runs does not raise peak VRAM at fixed concurrency. Residual: disk for ~62 runs; runtime amortizes after NVRTC warm; pairwise monotonicity/occupancy validation across full curve is validator scope.
ReadLints: no diagnostics on both files. Iteration implement->review = 1/3.
Evidence: Reviewed: git diff both files; orchestrator_limiter_v8.py L520-609,L770-967,L1806-1849; spawn_cap_ensemble.py full; spawn_cap_curve.py (synth_cumulative/synth_seed/get_reference_shape); rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7.py L282-312 (spawn_limit_active gating); invariants.json INV-2/INV-11/INV-13/GPU-*. Coder dispatch evidence: determinism EXCEPT=0 (master+repairline), monotonic births 0/30/60/61, cross-check 1060==vid9 EXCEPT=0, validated_ensemble_files=170, repair_quota=18.

Facts: Facts: context read: capsules limiter_v8/quota and config/transitions/invariants.json (INV-2/INV-3/INV-13, GPU-3). Phase_start written: ctx_W_ensemble_b3_2026-06-27_phase_start_d37df104. Dry-run/guard: spawn_cap_curve dry-run listed caps 0..60 + baseline vid1061; spawn_cap_ensemble --caps 61 --skip-uncapped failed fast with ValueError before model build. Main GPU sweep command: spawn_cap_ensemble.py --version-date 2026-06-22 --input-version-id 3 --out-base 1000 --uncapped-version-id 1061 --reference-version-id 9 --repair-quota 18 --concurrent-runs 4; PASS exit 0, CUDAEnsemble completed 62 runs, wall_s=150.544, ensemble_elapsed_s=150.409, loader_runs=62, validated_ensemble_files=2108, all final_days=3650, reference_shape threshold=9 days_total=4000, planned_spawn_disabled=1, deterministic_spawn_count=0, VRAM live approx 2.5-3.6 GiB. Temp determinism command: caps 30 60 out_base=1100 --skip-uncapped; PASS exit 0, CUDAEnsemble completed 2 runs. Loader: repair_quota=18 explicit for every load, sim_masterv2_v9 + sim_repairline_v9 inserted sequentially per vid, repairline_rows=65700 for each vid. SQL validation (ClickHouse 24.10.1.2812, version_date=20260622, group_by handled explicitly for births/deficit): births table has 62 rows and exact cap_identity 0..61; BIRTHS_CHECK monotonic=True cap_identity=True. Determinism EXCEPT=0 both directions for cap30 1030==1130 and cap60 1060==1160 on master+repairline. Ref births: vid4=55, vid5=56, vid6=57, vid7=58, vid8=59, vid9=60; bit-identity 1055..1060 vs vid4..9 EXCEPT=0 both directions on master+repairline. CSV_CHECK path=output/spawn_cap_curve_b3_20260622.csv rows=62 columns_ok=1 deficit_post180_nonincreasing=True unmet_hours_post180_nonincreasing=True; post180 deficit/unmet_hours are 0 across excerpt/all rows. handoffs.evidence:
Facts: CH version 24.10.1.2812. Schemas checked for sim_masterv2_v9, sim_repairline_v9, sim_deficit_v9_daily, flight_program_ac. cap48 vid1048 has 48 dynamic Mi-17 births; cap49 vid1049 has 49. First 48 births are identical by aircraft_number/day_u16/day_date; only extra is ac100048 at day_u16=2536 date=2033-06-01. deficit_post180 group_by=2: vid1048=744, vid1049=1160, net +416. Positive diff intervals: 2035-06-01..2035-09-28 sum +424 over 120 days, and 2036-06-01..2036-06-19 sum +55 over 19 days; negative offsets: 2033-06-01..2033-06-30 sum -30 and 2033-09-29..2033-10-31 sum -33. Repairline POS_MAIN: vid1048 busy avg 6.55/min2/max8; vid1049 avg 11.03/min5/max13; busy diff +3..+5, with existing aircraft occupying additional lines (e.g. line3 0->25828 for 120 days, lines5/6 0->22435/22504 for 114 days); extra ac100048 never appears in sim_repairline_v9. POS_TAIL: line15 0->100007 for 18 sampled repairline days. flight_program_ac version_date=2026-06-22 has version_ids 1..9, each 4000 unique dense daily dates 2026-06-22..2037-06-03, missing=0, max_gap=1. sim_deficit_v9_daily rows for vids1048/1049: 3650 rows per group_by, 7300 per vid for group_by 1+2, date range 2026-06-23..2036-06-19, zero target days=0. Deficit target exactly matches flight_program_ac version_id=3 ops_counter_mi8/mi17 for group_by 1/2, target_mismatch=0; vid1048 group_by=2 target has 70 non-zero segments and matches FP version_id=3 segments exactly.
Evidence: SELECT evidence: DESCRIBE TABLE on all four tables; births query grouped by version_id, aircraft_number with min(day_u16/day_date) for aircraft_number>=100000 and group_by=2; deficit query over sim_deficit_v9_daily for vids1048/1049 group_by=2 with Python interval aggregation; repairline query over sim_repairline_v9 for windows 2035-06-01..2035-09-28, 2036-06-01..2036-06-19, 2033-06-01..2033-06-30, 2033-09-29..2033-10-31; flight_program_ac density query for version_date=toDate('2026-06-22') and exact target LEFT JOIN check against version_id=3. Verdict Goal1: spawn cap boundary bug not supported; cap49 equals cap48 plus one final birth, and anomaly is a deterministic emergent timing/scheduling effect in repairline utilization. Caveat: causal mechanism traced from telemetry, not from code path inspection. Verdict Goal2: forward-fill and exact inner join are equivalent for this source because flight_program_ac version_id=3 is dense daily over the deficit horizon; no evidence of target loss/zeroing.

handoffs.facts:
SuccessCriteria: full command E2E phases measured; ensemble E2E vs single-run x62 compared; births linearity checked for version_date=20260622, vid1000..1061, group_by IN (1,2).

Phase table (cr=4):
- env/data prep to ensemble start: 9.850s; build_model registration inside it: 0.087s. Note: .rtc_cache already had 1606 files, so this is not a clean-cache cold NVRTC compile; any first-run JIT/cache lookup is included in ensemble wall.
- ensemble GPU wall: 136.596s (ensemble.getEnsembleElapsedTime=136.476s), 62/62 completed.
- post-GPU export validation gap before first loader line: 6.571s.
- loader .bin->CH master+repairline, 62 vid: sum total_s=369.636s; load_build=7.804s; master insert=155.100s; repairline=206.736s; avg=5.962s/vid.
- materializer daily+deficit, 62 vid: sum elapsed=209.390s; avg=3.377s/vid. Server system.query_log is absent, so daily vs deficit internal split cannot be recovered without modifying materializer; command materialized both tables per vid.
- E2E full command: 744.895s (12.415 min), return_code=0.

Comparison:
- single-run x62 with deficit: 62*13.35s=827.700s; ensemble E2E speedup=1.111x; absolute saving=82.805s.
- single-run x62 without deficit: 62*10.18s=631.160s; ensemble E2E is 1.180x slower than no-deficit extrapolation (+113.735s).
- effective ensemble E2E per run: 12.014s vs 13.35s single warm with deficit.
- effective GPU ensemble per run: 2.203s.
- bottleneck: post-GPU CH path dominates. Loader+materializer sum=579.026s (~77.7% of E2E) vs GPU wall=136.596s (~18.3% of E2E); largest subphase is loader total, especially repairline+master insert.

GPU utilization from harness nvidia-smi live: 137 samples; avg util=60.7%, max=80%; avg memory=1942 MiB, max=1991 MiB; before=13%,866 MiB; after=0%,1570 MiB.

Correctness evidence:
- SQL DESCRIBE checked required fields for sim_masterv2_v9, sim_repairline_v9, sim_masterv2_v9_daily, sim_deficit_v9_daily.
- SQL births rows=62; mismatches=[]; summary count=62, min_births=0, max_births=61, planned_mi17_total=0, mi8_births_total=0, all_pass=True.
- First rows: vid1000..1009 births 0..9; last rows: vid1052..1061 births 52..61.
- Row counts scoped to version_date=20260622, vid1000..1061, group_by IN (1,2): master=4,656,522 rows/62 versions; repairline=1,676,512 rows/62 versions; daily=2,715,600 rows/62 versions; deficit=452,600 rows/62 versions.
- Existing output/spawn_cap_curve_b3_20260622.csv also records cap,births as 0..61, matching the re-run.
handoffs.assumptions:
- Clean-cache cold NVRTC compile was not forced because deleting .rtc_cache was not explicitly requested; Risks if false: clean-cache compile overhead may be higher than the measured warm-cache build/first-run overhead.
- Materializer elapsed is daily+deficit combined from existing script logs; Risks if false: deficit-only share cannot be isolated from this run because system.query_log is absent.

handoffs.evidence:
Facts: Dispatch SuccessCriteria –њ—А–Њ–≤–µ—А–µ–љ –њ–Њ –і–Є—Д—Д—Г+SQL, verdict=ACCEPT. (1) –Р–і–і–Є—В–Є–≤–љ–Њ—Б—В—М single: materialize_daily([vid]) –Є–і—С—В DROP PARTITIONвЖТINSERT (–Ї–∞–Ї —А–∞–љ—М—И–µ), _multi_version_query([vid]) –і–∞—С—В 'version_id IN (vid)' —Н–Ї–≤–Є–≤ 'version_id = vid'; loader single (--version-id) вЖТ parallel_workers=1, load_version(delete_existing_master=True) —Н–Ї–≤–Є–≤ –њ—А–µ–ґ–љ–Є–є insert_master(deleteвЖТinsert). classic single-run –љ–µ –Ј–∞—В—А–Њ–љ—Г—В. (2) Parallel loader: _split_version_ids round-robin вЖТ –Ї–∞–ґ–і—Л–є worker —Г–љ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—М–љ—Л–µ version_id (–љ–µ–њ–µ—А–µ—Б–µ–Ї–∞—О—Й–Є–µ—Б—П –њ–∞—А—В–Є—Ж–Є–Є, –љ–µ—В write-–Ї–Њ–љ—Д–ї–Є–Ї—В–∞); –Ї–∞–ґ–і—Л–є worker —Б–≤–Њ–є get_client(); master DELETE –Њ–і–Є–љ —А–∞–Ј –≤–њ–µ—А—С–і (mutations_sync=2) –і–Њ –њ—Г–ї–∞, workers —В–Њ–ї—М–Ї–Њ INSERT; reconstruction –Є–Ј .bin –љ–µ –Є–Ј–Љ–µ–љ—С–љ. Expected: vid N вЖТ births=(N-1000) for 1000..1060, vid1061вЖТ61. Matches output/spawn_cap_curve_b3_20260622.csv.

Repairline sim_repairline_v9: row_count 1060=65700, vid9=65700; SELECT * EXCEPT both directions=0; loader-projection EXCEPT both=0; uniqExact(line_id) 1060=18, vid9=18.

CHECK-3 DEFICIT POST180 (PASS): Method spawn_cap_curve _metrics_for_version вАФ sum greatest(deficit,0) from sim_deficit_v9_daily group_by=2 where day_date.toordinal()>=2026-06-22+180 (2026-12-19). Cross-check ens vs ref vs expected:
1055/4=607/607/607 PASS; 1056/5=496/496/496 PASS; 1057/6=386/386/386 PASS; 1058/7=276/276/276 PASS; 1059/8=184/184/184 PASS; 1060/9=92/92/92 PASS.

handoffs.evidence_pack:
Facts: Primary LimiterV8/ensemble HostFunction inventory with citations:
1) HF_EnsemblePopulate: layer_ensemble_populate addHostFunction at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:719-727; class/run at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:397-474. Effective ensemble init/population; uses FLAMEGPU.agent(...).newAgent() for HELI/QuotaManager/RepairLine/spawn tickets; guarded by ensemble_pop_inited.
2) HF_InitMP5Cumsum: bound at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:729-732; class/run code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:1778-1803. Loads mp5_cumsum MacroProperty; guarded.
3) HF_InitSpawnLimitCumulative: bound at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:734-736; class/run code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:1806-1849. Loads/synthesizes spawn_limit_cumulative; guarded.
4) HF_InitEconomicsDailyCosts: bound at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:738-740; class/run code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:1852-1882. Loads daily economics MacroProperties; guarded.
5) HF_InitRepairLines: bound at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:750-754; class/run code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:1885-1977. Initializes repair_line_* MacroProperties/day-0 line state; guarded.
6) HF_InitV8: bound at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:756-759; class/run code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_v8.py:74-123. Loads current_day_mp/deterministic_dates_mp/adaptive_result_mp/mp_min_limiter; source comment says CUDASimulation lacks MacroProperty API and HostFunction.environment is used at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_v8.py:74-75.
7) HF_StepController: bound at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:762-767; class/run code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_optimized.py:333-426. Effective per-step controller: reads/resets mp_min_limiter, scans deterministic_dates_mp, computes adaptive_days, writes current_day_mp/adaptive_result_mp/mp2_day_for_step/mp2_num_steps, sets env prev_day/current_day/adaptive_days/step_days.
8) HF_DeterministicSpawn: conditionally bound at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:772-798; class/run code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:1646-1775. Per-step host layer; on due days creates serviceable HELI via svc_api.newAgent().
9) HF_MP2_Drain/HF_MP2_BinExport: addExitFunction at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_mp2_export.py:329-335 from code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:923-935. Drain reads numpy in non-ensemble, BinExport exports .bin in ensemble.
10) HF_RepairLineDrain/HF_RepairLineBinExport: addExitFunction at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_repairline_export.py:142-148 from code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:937-940. Drain reads numpy in non-ensemble, BinExport exports .bin in ensemble.
11) HF_ExitConditionV8: addExitCondition at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:959-961; class/run code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_v8.py:130-140.
Defined but not bound in primary V8: HF_InitMinLimiter/HF_ComputeAdaptiveDays at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_optimized.py:257-330; V8 commented out register_limiter_optimized at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:907-913.
Secondary messaging outside LimiterV8: InitPlanerSignalsHostFunction layer at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_units_v1.py:77-145; MP2DrainUnitsHostFunction addStepFunction at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_units_v1.py:316-327 and code/sim_v2/messaging/mp2_drain_units_v1.py:240-253. code/sim_env_setup.py has no host registration, only comment at code/sim_env_setup.py:1018-1027.
Per-step host callbacks in primary V8 simulate(): 7 unconditional layer HostFunction callbacks per step, + HF_DeterministicSpawn when spawn_schedule exists, + HF_ExitConditionV8 HostCondition. Effective heavy per-step host work is HF_StepController and conditional deterministic spawn; guarded init/population HFs still cross Python but return after first effective run.
Adaptive step: deterministic_dates assembled at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:582-620 and ensemble repair exits at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:716-718; HF_StepController advances by min(mp_min_limiter, days_to_next_deterministic_date) at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_optimized.py:345-413. Exact final_steps requires a run; code prints it at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:997-1004 and num_steps at code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py:1015-1033. Static order: code/model_build.py:28-30 sets MAX_EXPORT_STEPS=500 and comments typical 250-300, so typical per-run host layer callbacks are ~1750-2100 without deterministic spawn or ~2000-2400 with deterministic spawn, plus ~250-300 HostCondition calls.
Spawn: deterministic spawn is host-side newAgent; dynamic V8 spawn is device RTC via newRTCFunction/setAgentOutput at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7.py:638-657, with host pre-sim manager/ticket population at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7.py:697-731 and agent_out writes at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_spawn_dynamic_v7.py:141-229 and :340-423.
Quota/repair-line claims are device RTC: quota layers at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_quota_v8.py:1142-1262; RepairLine layers at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_repair_lines_v8.py:151-180; MacroProperty exchange/CAS-like claim code at code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_quota_v8.py:590-608, :655-708, :793-805.
Conclusion from code only: source confirms primary V8 simulate() is not GPU-only and enters Python host every model step. Source alone does not prove wall-time dominance vs GPU kernels. Necessarily host in current implementation: Python snapshot population/newAgent, env.setPropertyUInt synchronization in StepController, HostCondition termination, exit file/numpy export. Transfer candidates: StepController arithmetic/reduction/date scan and MP2 step mapping if current_day/prev_day/adaptive_days move to MacroProperty/device state and exit is redesigned; dynamic spawn/quota/repair-line claims already device RTC.
Evidence: Read-only source evidence only; no simulation, SQL, profiling, or code mutation performed. Searches covered code/sim_v2/messaging/*.py and code/sim_env_setup.py for host registration patterns and HostFunction/HostCondition subclasses. Agent KG phase_start context: ctx_W_ensemble_perf_2026-06-28_phase_start_eccfb448.

handoffs.assumptions: FLAME getElapsedTimeSimulation() was requested as GPU timing, but pyflamegpu docstring says it is duration of last simulate() call in seconds and observed value equals wall_s within ~9 microseconds. Risks if false: treating it as pure kernel time would double-count with measured Python host_total. Verdict therefore uses directly measured host_total/wall and reports FLAME elapsed separately, without claiming a pure kernel-only denominator.
handoffs.evidence:

(5) Path: tempfile.gettempdir()+–і–µ—В–µ—А–Љ.–Є–Љ—П(label/len/digest), –љ–µ –Ј–∞—Е–∞—А–і–Ї–Њ–ґ–µ–љ–Њ; tmp+replace; size fail-fast. Pre-L1 references from user: cr=4=128.7, cr=6=165.0, cr=8=184.5. Selected optimum: cr=4.
Full E2E L1 at cr=4 for vid1000..1061: /usr/bin/time real=298.95s user=286.05 sys=41.78; GPU wall_s=109.338 ensemble_elapsed_s=109.229; loader=97.393s; materializer=81.890s; load_total=179.283s; derived build/orchestration/validation overhead = 298.95 - 109.338 - 97.393 - 81.890 = 10.329s; validated_ensemble_files=2108. Baseline E2E before L1: 308.5s, so full-pipeline gain = 9.55s (3.1%). GPU phase cr=4 gain vs pre-L1 = 19.855s (15.4%).

Correctness PASS.

changes: Diagnostic measurement only. No persistent code instrumentation was written to code/**: profiling was done via in-memory monkey-patching of HostFunction/HostCondition run() methods in a standalone runner. No commit. No git restore was required because code/sim_v2/messaging remained diff-clean throughout; only config/agent_kg.json changed due KG context/handoff writes.
assumptions:
Assumption: prior after-L1 wall reference cr4=108.845s and cr8=120.884s remains the operator comparison point for the ~12s gap. Risks if false: the 20/80 split against reference gap changes, but the instrumented run still shows host delta=2.455s and FLAME per-run timing/util trend.
Assumption: ExitLogFrame.getTotalTime is the relevant FLAME programmatic per-run timing requested as FLAME GPU-time. Risks if false: a lower-level CUDA kernel-only timer would need nsys/CUPTI; current evidence still distinguishes host wrapper time from FLAME internal timing.
Assumption: In-memory monkey-patch timing overhead is small enough for relative attribution. Risks if false: host total is slightly inflated; this would strengthen, not weaken, the conclusion that host cannot explain the full prior ~12s gap.
evidence:
Scope: GPU-only full sweep caps 0..60 + uncapped 1061, version_date=2026-06-22, input_version_id=3, out_base=1000, repair_quota=18, ClickHouse load skipped. Instrumented targets: HF_EnsemblePopulate, HF_InitMP5Cumsum, HF_InitSpawnLimitCumulative, HF_InitEconomicsDailyCosts, HF_InitRepairLines, HF_InitV8, HF_StepController, HF_ExitConditionV8. Thread-safe accumulation via threading.Lock. FLAME timing: CUDAEnsemble Config().timing=True + LoggingConfig.logTiming(True); used ExitLogFrame getTotalTime/getRTCTime/getInitTime/getExitTime. nvidia-smi sampler: utilization.gpu, utilization.memory, clocks.sm at -lms 200.
Results: cr=4 wall=89.245s ensemble_elapsed=89.031s summed_host=6.792s host_calls=122192 FLAME_total_sum=325.118s FLAME_total_avg=5.244s FLAME_rtc_sum=24.739s util_avg=66.48 util_max=75. cr=6 wall=89.920s ensemble_elapsed=89.918s summed_host=7.947s host_calls=122192 FLAME_total_sum=500.649s FLAME_total_avg=8.075s FLAME_rtc_sum=28.326s util_avg=67.26 util_max=72. cr=8 wall=92.578s ensemble_elapsed=92.574s summed_host=9.246s host_calls=122192 FLAME_total_sum=686.109s FLAME_total_avg=11.066s FLAME_rtc_sum=26.191s util_avg=63.94 util_max=70. All run_count=62, final_days_min=max=3650, step_count_sum=15274.
Host by name cr4: StepController=2.728s, EnsemblePopulate=1.474s, InitEconomics=0.998s, InitMP5=0.555s, InitRepairLines=0.432s, ExitCondition=0.328s, InitSpawnLimit=0.223s, InitV8=0.054s. Host by name cr8: StepController=3.404s, EnsemblePopulate=1.974s, InitEconomics=1.793s, InitMP5=0.593s, InitRepairLines=0.645s, ExitCondition=0.407s, InitSpawnLimit=0.369s, InitV8=0.061s.
Decomposition: instrumented cr8-cr4 wall gap=3.333s; tracked host delta=2.455s (73.7%), residual=0.878s (26.3%). Against the prior standard after-L1 gap from user/harness (120.884-108.845=12.039s), the same measured host delta explains only 2.455s (20.4%), leaving 9.584s (79.6%) for FLAME/GPU scheduling/output/other overhead. FLAME total avg per run rises 5.244 -> 11.066s while GPU util avg drops 66.48% -> 63.94% and util max drops 75 -> 70.
Artifacts: output/crprofile_20260629/summary.json; output/crprofile_20260629/build.log; output/crprofile_20260629/sim_cr4.log; sim_cr6.log; sim_cr8.log. Clean rollback/working-tree check: git diff --stat -- code/sim_v2/messaging produced no entries; git status --short shows only M config/agent_kg.json.
risk_reasons: Measurement of simulation runtime behavior; diagnostic instrumentation around HostFunction/HostCondition; full GPU-only sweep
plan_card: Instrument host functions in-memory; run GPU-only full sweep cr=4/6/8 with FLAME timing and nvidia-smi; decompose cr8-cr4 gap; verify no persistent code diff.

evidence:
Stage 1 config: version_date=2026-06-22, input_version_id=3, out_base=1000, caps 0..60 + uncapped 1061, repair_quota=18, end_day=3650, cr=4, GPU-only, no ClickHouse load/materialize. Same model/specs reused; each mode creates fresh RunPlanVector/CUDAEnsemble and measures only ensemble.simulate(). Build stdout redirected outside measured interval.
Measurements: (a) normal_stdout wall_s=87.557 ensemble_elapsed_s=87.333; (b) noop_print via temporary builtins.print no-op wall_s=87.492 ensemble_elapsed_s=87.490; (c) stdout_file via contextlib.redirect_stdout(file) wall_s=87.183 ensemble_elapsed_s=87.182. All modes run_count=62, final_day_min=max=3650, step_count_sum=15274.
Deltas: normal - noop_print = 0.065s; normal - stdout_file = 0.374s. This is far below significance thresholds (>5s requested for implementation, <~3s no-op). Nature: Python print formatting/write cost is effectively negligible in this run; terminal/rendering component, if any, is also sub-second here. The earlier ~20s difference is not reproduced by isolated print suppression and is likely from other harness/profiling differences/noise, not per-run setup print().

GIL-held: cr1 5.93%, cr2 6.54%, cr4 6.33%, cr6 6.74%, cr8 7.48%; all <40%. HF_StepController mapped samples (rtc_limiter_optimized.py:340-426): 21,26,22,24,28 samples = ~0.50-0.76% of profiled wall. Top GIL functions are preload_mp5_maps/read_items/build_mp5_linear/compute_mp5_cumsum plus small pyflamegpu setup samples. Likely bottleneck is GPU/driver-side scheduling/stream overlap on one device, not Python GIL.

assumptions: Nsight correlationId join from CUPTI kernel to runtime globalTid represents run host-thread grouping; Risks if false: run-group overlap should be interpreted as host-thread overlap, while streamId overlap metrics still remain valid.
evidence:
Facts: Existing traces were sufficient; no new nsys run executed. Initial sweep implementation was corrected to event-line ordering to avoid counting idle gaps as active. Verdict: not B (no device-wide sync); not literal A (cr8 has 8 host threads and 56 streams); dominant C-like serialization with very small/frequent kernels plus per-stream synchronization/API cadence, because run-group overlap remains only 9.50% and avg active run groups only 1.10.
Evidence: SQLite schema: CUPTI_ACTIVITY_KIND_KERNEL has start,end,deviceId,contextId,streamId,globalPid,demangledName,shortName,etc.; runtime has start,end,eventClass,globalTid,correlationId,nameId. Counts: all cr have 245253 kernels, 56 streamId, 1 context, 1 device, 1 globalPid. Host threads via correlationId: cr1=1, cr4=4, cr8=8; cr8 each thread uses 7 streams. Sync: cudaDeviceSynchronize*=0; cudaStreamSynchronize*=1493090 calls (cr1 0.808s, cr4 3.106s, cr8 3.308s). Corrected event-line overlap by globalTid: cr1 max=1 avg=1.000 overlap=0%; cr8 max=5 avg=1.103 overlap=9.503% of kernel-active time. By streamId: cr1 max=4 avg=1.085 overlap=6.610%; cr8 max=7 avg=1.104 overlap=9.551%. Kernel durations cr8 avg=10.130us p50=2.144us p90=23.456us p99=144.478us.
risk_reasons: Read-only profiling analysis in CUDA/FLAME runtime; no code changes; interpretation affects performance diagnosis.

Workflow GPU-only day-loop. –¶–µ–ї—М: —Г–±—А–∞—В—М per-step Python host-callback'–Є –Є–Ј CUDAEnsemble day-loop (—Б–µ—А–Є–∞–ї–Є–Ј—Г—О—В—Б—П –љ–∞ GIL, –ї–Њ–Љ–∞—О—В –Љ–∞—Б—И—В–∞–±–Є—А–Њ–≤–∞–љ–Є–µ –њ–Њ concurrent_runs). –Ч–Р–Ф–Р–І–Р P1b: –њ–µ—А–µ–љ–µ—Б—В–Є HF_StepController (rtc_limiter_optimized.py class ~333, run ~340) —Б Python-host –љ–∞ device RTC, —З—В–Њ–±—Л –£–С–†–Р–Ґ–ђ per-step host-callback –Я–Ю–Ы–Э–Ю–°–Ґ–ђ–Ѓ.
changes:
DESIGN:
- Candidate carrier: existing QuotaManager singleton pair, execute RTC StepController only for group_by==1 via RTC function condition. This avoids new agent types and preserves exactly-one execution per step.
- Device StepController logic would read current_day_mp[0], end_day env, mp_min_limiter[0], deterministic_dates_mp, num_deterministic_dates; reset mp_min_limiter[0] with exchange(0xFFFFFFFF); write current_day_mp[0/1], adaptive_result_mp[0/1], mp2_day_for_step[step], mp2_num_steps[0].
- Layer order would keep layer_step_controller first, before save_pre_status/state/quota/spawn/repairline readers.
- Env consumers: HF_DeterministicSpawn should read current_day_mp instead of env current_day; spawn_cap_ensemble exit report should stop depending on stale env current_day or be handled by one final/non-step host sync.
BLOCKERS:
- Strict SuccessCriteria says ZERO per-step Python host-callback. Current active model still registers rtc_limiter_v8.HF_ExitConditionV8 via model.addExitCondition in orchestrator_limiter_v8.py. pyflamegpu exposes addExitCondition as HostConditionCallback; this is host/Python control-plane code evaluated per step.
- Removing only HF_StepController would still leave the per-step HostCondition callback, so it does not satisfy the user's zero-callback criterion.
- Removing HostCondition without a device-side stop/request-exit mechanism would force runs to continue to max_steps after current_day>=end_day, preserving neither performance intent nor clean adaptive loop semantics.
RECOMMENDATION:
- Do not implement P1b as a partial StepController-only change under the stated SuccessCriteria.
- First decide an exit strategy: confirm a supported device-side termination API in FLAME GPU 2 rc4, or redesign harness/run plans so termination is non-Python and does not execute empty post-end steps. After that, implement device StepController and env-consumer migration together.
assumptions: Assumption: FLAME GPU 2 rc4 has no project-used device-side request-exit API available for RTC layer termination. Risks if false: implementation can proceed after confirming the exact API and removing HostCondition.
evidence:
Facts:
- Read config/capsules_manifest.json, docs/limiter_v8_capsule.md, docs/flame_gpu_capsule.md, docs/quota_capsule.md, config/transitions/invariants.json.
- Verified HF_StepController in code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_optimized.py: reads env current_day/end_day, resets mp_min_limiter, computes deterministic-date clamp, writes current_day_mp/adaptive_result_mp/env/mp2 mapping.
- Verified layer_step_controller in code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py currently addHostFunction(HF_StepController) before V8 readers.
- Verified QuotaManager has exactly two agents group_by=1/2 in single-run and ensemble population paths.
- Verified active host env consumer HF_DeterministicSpawn reads env current_day; spawn_cap_ensemble logs env current_day for final_days.
- Verified active exit callback: orchestrator_limiter_v8.py registers rtc_limiter_v8.HF_ExitConditionV8 via model.addExitCondition; pyflamegpu include ModelDescription.h stores addExitCondition callbacks as HostConditionCallback.
- No code edits were made; no golden/perf runs were executed because design gate failed strict zero-callback.
risk_reasons: code/sim_v2/messaging day-loop architecture; CUDAEnsemble host-callback scaling; step/exit semantics affect adaptive simulation outputs
plan_card: Design/feasibility only. Device StepController on QuotaManager(group_by=1) is technically feasible for day advance and MP writes, but strict zero per-step host-callback is blocked by existing Python HostCondition exit callback.

open_questions: Is HostCondition counted in the workflow's zero per-step host-callback definition? Is there a supported pyflamegpu rc4 device-side simulation termination API that can replace HF_ExitConditionV8?

user_goal: –І–µ–ї–Њ–≤–µ–Ї –Њ—Б–ї–∞–±–Є–ї –Ї—А–Є—В–µ—А–Є–є. –†–Х–Р–Ы–Ш–Ч–£–Щ P1b (–љ–µ —В–Њ–ї—М–Ї–Њ design). –¶–µ–ї—М: —Г–±—А–∞—В—М –Ґ–ѓ–Ц–Б–Ы–Ђ–Щ per-step host-callback HF_StepController, –њ–µ—А–µ–љ–µ—Б—П –µ–≥–Њ –љ–∞ device RTC; HF_ExitConditionV8 –Њ—Б—В–∞–≤–Є—В—М host; bit-identical 68/68; scaling cr62/cr4.
changes:
- code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_optimized.py: added device StepController as two QuotaManager RTC layers: prepare reads current_day_mp/mp_min_limiter/deterministic_dates into QuotaManager scratch; commit writes current_day_mp/adaptive_result_mp/mp_min_limiter reset/mp2_day_for_step/mp2_num_steps. Both functions have RTC function condition group_by==1 plus internal guard, so exactly one QuotaManager agent executes the controller.
- code/sim_v2/messaging/base_model_messaging.py: added QuotaManager scratch variables step_prev_day, step_new_day, step_adaptive_days, step_min_limiter.
- code/sim_v2/messaging/orchestrator_limiter_v8.py: replaced layer_step_controller host function with register_device_step_controller; HF_DeterministicSpawn now reads current_day_mp[0] instead of env current_day; registered one-time exit function for legacy env current_day logging.
- code/sim_v2/messaging/rtc_limiter_v8.py: added HF_SyncDayEnvOnExit, an ExitFunction that syncs env current_day/prev_day/adaptive_days/step_days once at simulation exit for LoggingConfig.
- code/utils/spawn_cap_ensemble.py: added explicit --threshold option so the B3b harness can use the human-specified threshold=9 without requiring nonexistent heli_pandas reference version ids; reference-version path remains default when --threshold is absent.
assumptions: Assumption: end_day=3650 is the correct golden horizon despite user text mentioning days_total=4000, because P0/P1a KG evidence and golden replay final_days are 3650 while shape days_total is 4000. Risks if false: the 4000-day run is a different acceptance oracle and currently mismatches 52/68.
evidence:
Facts:
- Single-exec mechanism: existing QuotaManager has two agents group_by=1/2; both StepController RTC functions use setRTCFunctionCondition(cond_step_controller_qm_mi8) and an internal group_by!=1 early return. The read and write phases are split to satisfy FLAME GPU MacroProperty read/write rules.
- Env consumers: HF_DeterministicSpawn reads current_day_mp[0]; env current_day/prev_day/adaptive_days/step_days are no longer written per step by StepController. Existing spawn_cap_ensemble exit-log remains on env current_day, fed by one-time HF_SyncDayEnvOnExit ExitFunction. HF_ExitConditionV8 remains host by accepted criterion and already reads current_day_mp.
- Active env-day grep: remaining env current_day reads in messaging are legacy/unregistered V7 or old limiter functions, except one-time exit sync writes; active V8 post-quota paths use current_day_mp. register_post_quota_v7 active functions already read current_day_mp.
- Compile/lint: python3 -m py_compile modified files PASS; ReadLints no errors; git diff --check PASS.
- B3b golden: command with version_date=2026-06-22 input_version_id=1 caps=0,7 out_base=13000 threshold=9 repair_quota=18 concurrent_runs=2 skip_load. Initial end_day=4000 produced 52 mismatches because golden baseline is final_days=3650; Agent KG P0/P1a evidence confirms baseline final_days=3650 with days_total shape=4000. Re-run with end_day=3650 matched golden.
- Golden compare: output/p0_dayloop_golden_hashes.json vs regenerated output/ensemble_b2 run_13000/run_13007 => total=68 matched=68 missing=0 mismatches=0.
- Scaling command: output/gpu_hypothesis_profile.py --mode wall --concurrent-runs 4 62 --version-date 2026-06-22 --input-version-id 1 --threshold 180 --out-base 9900 --uncapped-version-id 9961 --repair-quota 18. Result output/gpu_hypothesis_wall.json: cr4 wall=89.039250699s, cr62 wall=119.145807822s, ratio=1.338. Baseline ratio ~1.34, so the expected noticeable gap reduction was NOT observed.
risk_reasons: code/sim_v2/messaging adaptive day-loop; CUDAEnsemble per-step control flow; MP2/RL export hashes are acceptance oracle
plan_card: Implement device StepController and remove heavy per-step HF_StepController while keeping accepted host HF_ExitConditionV8. Validate B3b sha256 and run cr4/cr62 scaling profile.
